title: mls: process_for_group returns committer pub_id for Commit messages; callers may misattribute
description: In mls.rs process_for_group, sender_pub_id is extracted from processed.credential() before the match on processed.into_content(). For ApplicationMessage the sender_pub_id is meaningful and returned. For StagedCommitMessage and ProposalMessage, the function returns Ok(None), but the sender_pub_id was still computed and not returned — that is fine. However, the credential for a Commit message is the committer's identity, which is correct. BUT: there is no validation that sender_pub_id matches a known/expected identity format. The credential bytes are user-supplied at group-creation time; a malicious joiner could craft a credential with pub_id bytes that are not a valid hex pub_id (not SHA-256 of their actual pub key). The relay does enforce that the wire-protocol pub_id matches the Ed25519 key, but MLS credentials are set by the joining client and the relay does not see them. A malicious client could set an MLS credential that spoofs another user's pub_id. This would be caught only if receivers independently validate that MLS sender_pub_id == authenticated relay pub_id.
